title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: metadata parquet files dataset can downloaded Zenodo GitHub. simplify DeconvolutionLabel1 column use input cell type labels. contains cell types 8-μ\mum binned data predicted deconvolution using single-cell RNA-seq reference. input data frame SCOPE contain following columns: CellID: cell id ImageID: sample id X: X coordinate Y: Y coordinate Celltype: cell type labels remove bins assigned “doublets” “rejected” deconvolution algorithm focus singlet bins.
code: CRC_VisiumHD_metadata <- sapply(c("P1", "P2", "P5"), function(x) {   read_parquet(paste0("https://github.com/10XGenomics/HumanColonCancer_VisiumHD/",                       "raw/refs/heads/main/MetaData/", x, "CRC_Metadata.parquet"))   }, simplify = FALSE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) %>%   data.table::rbindlist(idcol = "ImageID") %>%   # Make cell ids unique   mutate(barcode = paste(barcode, ImageID, sep = "_")) %>%   # Simplifying cell type annotations   mutate(Celltype = case_when(     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Mature B", "Memory B") ~ "B cell",       DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Proliferating Macrophages",                                 "Proliferating Immune II") ~ "Prolif_Immune",     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Fibroblast", "Vascular Fibroblast",                                 "Proliferating Fibroblast") ~ "Fibroblast",      DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Enteric Glial", "Enterocyte", "Neuroendocrine") ~ "Neuronal",      str_detect(DeconvolutionLabel1, "Tumor") ~ "Tumor",     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Epithelial", "Tuft", "Goblet") ~ "Epithelial",      DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Smooth Muscle", "SM Stress Response", "vSM") ~ "Smooth Muscle",       DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Unknown III (SM)", "Adipocyte") ~ "Other",     TRUE ~ DeconvolutionLabel1   )) CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in <- CRC_VisiumHD_metadata %>%   # Keeping only the singlet bins   filter(DeconvolutionClass == "singlet") %>%   select(CellID = barcode, ImageID, X = X, Y = Y, Celltype = Celltype)  # save(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, file = paste0("data/", dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData")) head(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in) # 810,330 bins #>                      CellID ImageID        X         Y Celltype #>                      <char>  <char>    <num>     <num>   <char> #> 1: s_008um_00000_00289-1_P1      P1 69.20113 -309.4240    Tumor #> 2: s_008um_00000_00290-1_P1      P1 69.44765 -309.4263    Tumor #> 3: s_008um_00000_00291-1_P1      P1 69.69418 -309.4286    Tumor #> 4: s_008um_00000_00292-1_P1      P1 69.94070 -309.4309    Tumor #> 5: s_008um_00000_00293-1_P1      P1 70.18723 -309.4332    Tumor #> 6: s_008um_00000_00294-1_P1      P1 70.43375 -309.4354    Tumor CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = celltype_colors,                       breaks = names(celltype_colors)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank())

title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: Next, use k-nearest neighbors algorithm define spatial neighborhood compute neighborhood cell type composition matrix. default number nearest clusters kk 20, however can increase kk larger tissue slices. set kk=50. addition, enforce distance boundary nearest neighbors exclude cells falling outside radius. boundary initially defined 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range, reset either minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). can examine distribution 99th percentile distance nearest neighbors select threshold parameters min.radius max.radius accordingly. 99th percentiles 2.466, 1.679, 1.939 P1, P2, P5 respectively, percentiles remain nearly identical across three samples. Therefore set max.radius 2 min.radius 1.  Cells retain fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code: slide_list <- unique(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in$ImageID)  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist <- pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   slide_list, function(s) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=subset(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, ImageID == s),       knn_num = 50, cell_types_available = unique(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in$Celltype),       min.radius = 1,       # lower limit for the boundary       max.radius = 2,       # upper limit for the boundary       min.n.neighbors = 15, # minimum no. of neighbors       prob_list = seq(0, 1, 0.1))},   mc.cores = 3) # set mc.cores to 1 on Windows # save(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist,  #      file = paste0("data/", dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_results.RData")) length(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist) #> [1] 3 sapply(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "dist.quantiles") %>%   as.data.frame() %>%    rownames_to_column("perc") %>%   mutate(perc = as.numeric(str_remove(perc, "%"))) %>%   pivot_longer(2:ncol(.), names_to = "ImageID", values_to = "value") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = perc, y = value)) +   geom_point(aes(color = ImageID)) +   scale_color_discrete(labels = slide_list) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) {paste0(x, "%")}) +   labs(y = "Distance to\nNearest Neighbors", x = "Percentile") +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) # Combine neighborhood proportion table from the three samples cell_neighbor_table_maxdist <- lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "prop.df") %>%   do.call("rbind", .) cells_to_exclude = apply(cell_neighbor_table_maxdist, 1, function(x) {any(is.na(x))}) %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   `colnames<-`("is_na") %>%   rownames_to_column("CellID") %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in[, c("CellID", "X", "Y", "ImageID")], by = "CellID")  # Number of cells to exclude table(cells_to_exclude$is_na) #>  #>  FALSE   TRUE  #> 809156   1174  cells_to_exclude %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To\nExclude") +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank())

what: 3. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: Next, apply SCOPE B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} triad using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing driver cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 3 separate B cell T cell zones. can see B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} CTNs closely aligns lymphocyte aggregates CRC samples, CTN1 resembles B cell zone CTN2 resembles surrounding T cell zone.
code: driver_celltypes <- c("B cell", "CD4 T cell", "CD8 T cell")  CTN_df <- run_SCOPE(   combination_table = as.data.frame(matrix(driver_celltypes, nrow = 1)),   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in,   save_results = FALSE,    clusternum = 3, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) # save(CTN_df, file = paste0("data/", dataset_name, "_B_CD4T_CD8T.RData")) # Number of cells per CTN table(CTN_df$label) #>  #>       CTN1       CTN2 Unassigned  #>       9121       4242     795793 # CTN label with colored dots separating the cell types CTN_label <- color_CTN_names(   paste(driver_celltypes, collapse = "_"), celltype_colors)   # Visualizing B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell CTN CTN_df %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = label)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("CTN1" = "#E69F00", "CTN2" = "#56B4E9",                                  "Unassigned" = "grey90"), name = NULL) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank())  # Show driver cell types only CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in %>%   mutate(Celltype = ifelse(Celltype %in% driver_celltypes, Celltype, "Other")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = celltype_colors,                       breaks = names(celltype_colors)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = "Driver cell type triad") +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank()) CTN_df %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   count(Celltype, label) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = n, y = label, fill = factor(Celltype, names(celltype_colors)))) +   geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = TRUE)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = celltype_colors, name = NULL) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +   cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Proportion of Cells", y = NULL) +   theme(plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.location = "plot",         legend.justification = "right") sessionInfo() #> R version 4.4.1 (2024-06-14) #> Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20 #> Running under: macOS 15.7.5 #>  #> Matrix products: default #> BLAS:   /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.4-arm64/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib  #> LAPACK: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.4-arm64/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib;  LAPACK version 3.12.0 #>  #> locale: #> [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 #>  #> time zone: America/Chicago #> tzcode source: internal #>  #> attached base packages: #> [1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base      #>  #> other attached packages: #>  [1] arrow_22.0.0.1     cowplot_1.1.3      ggtext_0.1.2       lubridate_1.9.4    #>  [5] forcats_1.0.0      stringr_1.5.1      dplyr_1.1.4        purrr_1.0.4        #>  [9] readr_2.1.5        tidyr_1.3.1        tibble_3.2.1       ggplot2_4.0.2.9000 #> [13] tidyverse_2.0.0    data.table_1.17.0  SCOPE.CTN_0.0.1    #>  #> loaded via a namespace (and not attached): #>  [1] gtable_0.3.6       xfun_0.52          bslib_0.9.0        htmlwidgets_1.6.4  #>  [5] lattice_0.22-7     tzdb_0.5.0         vctrs_0.6.5        tools_4.4.1        #>  [9] generics_0.1.3     parallel_4.4.1     pbmcapply_1.5.1    pkgconfig_2.0.3    #> [13] Matrix_1.7-3       RColorBrewer_1.1-3 S7_0.2.1           desc_1.4.3 yaml_2.3.10        gmp_0.7-5.1        pillar_1.10.2      #> [29] pkgdown_2.1.1      jquerylib_0.1.4    cachem_1.1.0       nlme_3.1-168       #> [33] commonmark_1.9.5   tidyselect_1.2.1   digest_0.6.37      stringi_1.8.7      #> [37] labeling_0.4.3     splines_4.4.1      fastmap_1.2.0      grid_4.4.1         #> [41] cli_3.6.4          magrittr_2.0.3     withr_3.0.2        scales_1.4.0       #> [45] bit64_4.6.0-1      timechange_0.3.0   rmarkdown_2.29     bit_4.6.0          #> [49] ragg_1.4.0         hms_1.1.3          evaluate_1.0.3     knitr_1.50         #> [53] irlba_2.3.5.1      markdown_2.0 ClusterR_1.3.6     #> [65] systemfonts_1.3.1  fs_1.6.6

title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: rds file containing Seurat object dataset can downloaded Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) accesssion number GSE250346. also use cells_partitioned_by_annotation.csv file GSE250346_HE_annotations.tar.gz contains annotations PF histopathological features (including TLSs). samples file need renamed following code original paper: input data frame SCOPE contain following columns: CellID: cell id ImageID: sample id X: X coordinate Y: Y coordinate Celltype: cell type labels four samples containing TLSs:
code: PF_Xenium_obj <- readRDS("../data/GSE250346_Seurat_GSE250346_CORRECTED_SEE_RDS_README_082024.rds") clinical_data <- distinct(PF_Xenium_obj@meta.data, sample, sample_affect) save(clinical_data, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_clinical_data.RData"))) # load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_clinical_data.RData")) # PF_Xenium_annot <- read.csv(paste0(data_path, "cells_partitioned_by_annotation.csv")) %>% #   ## keeping only the relevant columns to reduce data size #   select(CellID = cell_id, ImageID = sample, Annotation_Type, annotation_type_instance) %>% #   mutate(patient = str_extract(ImageID, "(THD|TILD|VUHD|VUILD)[0-9]+")) %>% #   left_join(clinical_data[, c("patient", "sample_affect")], by = "patient") %>% #   ## Change sample name based on the code from the original paper ( #   ## Initial rename #   mutate(ImageID_new = case_when( #     sample_affect == "Less Affected" ~ paste0(patient, "LA"), #     sample_affect == "More Affected"  ~ paste0(patient, "MA"), #     TRUE ~ ImageID)) %>% #   ## Fix additional names #   mutate(ImageID_new = case_when( #     ImageID == "TILD117MF" ~ "TILD117MA1", #     ImageID == "TILD117MFB" ~ "TILD117MA2", #     ImageID == "VUILD104LF" ~ "VUILD104MA1", #     ImageID == "VUILD104MF" ~ "VUILD104MA2", #     ImageID == "VUILD105LF" ~ "VUILD105MA1", #     ImageID == "VUILD105MF" ~ "VUILD105MA2", #     ImageID == "VUILD48LF" ~ "VUILD48LA1", #     ImageID == "VUILD48MF" ~ "VUILD48LA2", #     str_detect(ImageID_new, 'LF$') ~ str_replace(ImageID_new, "LF$", "LA"), #     str_detect(ImageID_new, 'MF$') ~ str_replace(ImageID_new, "MF$", "MA"), #     TRUE ~ ImageID_new)) %>% #   mutate(CellID = paste(ImageID_new, CellID, sep = "_")) %>% #   select(-ImageID) %>% #   rename(ImageID = ImageID_new) # # save(PF_Xenium_annot, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_annotations.RData")) load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_annotations.RData")) PF_Xenium_dat_in <- PF_Xenium_obj@meta.data %>%   select(CellID = full_cell_id, ImageID = sample,           X = x_centroid, Y = y_centroid, Celltype = final_CT) ## save(PF_Xenium_dat_in, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData")) TLS_img_list <- unique(subset(PF_Xenium_annot, Annotation_Type == "TLS")$ImageID)  ## Images containing TLSs, colored by cell type PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% c(TLS_img_list)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette,                       breaks = names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom")  ## Images containing TLSs, colored by annotations PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_annot[, c("CellID", "Annotation_Type")], by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% c(TLS_img_list)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Annotation_Type)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(breaks = names(annotation_palette), na.value = "grey90",                      values = annotation_palette, name = NULL) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom")

title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: Next, use k-nearest neighbors algorithm define spatial neighborhood compute neighborhood cell type composition matrix. default number nearest clusters kk 20, however can increase kk larger tissues. Since dataset contains 3-5mm lung tissue cores, set k=50k=50. addition, enforce distance boundary nearest neighbors exclude cells falling outside radius. boundary initially defined 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range, reset either minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). can examine distribution 99th percentile distance nearest neighbors select threshold parameters min.radius max.radius accordingly. set max.radius min.radius default values (500 100 respectively).  can see two outlier samples represent sparsely populated regions tissue.  cells retaining fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary, neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code:
slide_list <- unique(PF_Xenium_dat_in$ImageID)  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist <- pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   slide_list, function(s) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=subset(PF_Xenium_dat_in, ImageID == s),       knn_num = 50, cell_types_available = unique(PF_Xenium_dat_in$Celltype),       min.radius = 100,       # lower limit for the boundary       max.radius = 500,       # upper limit for the boundary       min.n.neighbors = 10,   # minimum no. of neighbors,       return.nn.ids = TRUE,   # Whether to return nearest neighbor IDs       prob_list = seq(0, 1, 0.1))},   mc.cores = 4) # set mc.cores=1 on Windows machine # save(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, #      file = paste0("../data/", dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_results.RData")) ## 99th percentile of the distance to nearest neighbors dist_99th <- sapply(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "dist.quantiles") %>%   `colnames<-`(slide_list) %>%   as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("perc") %>%   mutate(perc = as.numeric(str_remove(perc, "%"))) %>%   pivot_longer(2:ncol(.), names_to = "ImageID", values_to = "value") %>%   filter(perc == 99)  ## Distribution of 99th percentile dist_99th %>%   ggplot(aes(x = value)) +   geom_histogram(color = "white", fill = "skyblue", bins = 20) +   geom_boxplot() +   geom_text(data = subset(dist_99th, value > 150),              aes(label = ImageID, y = 2),              hjust = 0, size = 3.5, angle = 90) +   labs(y = "99th percentile of Dist.
to Nearest Neighbors", x = NULL) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, angle = 45)) img_outliers <- arrange(subset(dist_99th, value > 150), value)$ImageID PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% img_outliers) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette,                       breaks = names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette),                      labels = rename_celltype, name = NULL) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 5)) +   facet_wrap(~ factor(ImageID, img_outliers), scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom") # Combine neighborhood proportion table cell_neighbor_table_maxdist <- lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "prop.df") %>%   do.call("rbind", .) cells_to_exclude = apply(cell_neighbor_table_maxdist, 1, function(x) {any(is.na(x))}) %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   `colnames<-`("is_na") %>%   rownames_to_column("CellID") %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in[, c("CellID", "X", "Y", "ImageID")], by = "CellID")  # Number of cells to exclude table(cells_to_exclude$is_na) #>  #>   FALSE    TRUE  #> 1628026    2293  # Cells to be removed from the two sparse samples cells_to_exclude %>%   filter(ImageID %in% img_outliers) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na, size = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To
Exclude") +   scale_size_manual(values = c(0.1, 1)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1), size = "none") +   facet_wrap(~factor(ImageID, img_outliers), scales = "free") +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         aspect.ratio = 1,         panel.background = element_blank()) # Cells to be removed from the samples containing TLSs cells_to_exclude %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na, size = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To
Exclude") +   scale_size_manual(values = c(0.1, 0.5)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1), size = "none") +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         aspect.ratio = 1,         panel.background = element_blank())

what: 3. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: Next, apply SCOPE B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} triad using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing driver cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 3 separate B cell T cell zones. can see B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} CTNs closely aligns lymphocyte aggregates CRC samples, CTN1 resembles B cell zone CTN2 resembles surrounding T cell zone.   can compute evaluation metrics comparing CTN1 ground truth TLS annotations
code: driver_celltypes <- c("B cells", "CD4+ T-cells", "CD8+ T-cells")  CTN_df <- run_SCOPE(   combination_table = as.data.frame(matrix(driver_celltypes, nrow = 1)),   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = PF_Xenium_dat_in,   save_results = FALSE,    clusternum = 3, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) # save(CTN_df, file = paste0("../data/", dataset_name, "_B_CD4T_CD8T.RData")) # Number of cells per CTN table(CTN_df$label) #>  #>       CTN1       CTN2 Unassigned  #>      36343      51050    1540633 # CTN label with colored dots separating the cell types CTN_label <- color_CTN_names(   paste(driver_celltypes, collapse = "_"), PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)   # Visualizing B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell CTN CTN_df %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = label)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("CTN1" = "#E69F00", "CTN2" = "#56B4E9",                                  "Unassigned" = "grey90"), name = NULL) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1)  # Show driver cell types only PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   filter(CellID %in% CTN_df$CellID) %>% # dropping cells with few neighbors   mutate(Celltype = ifelse(Celltype %in% driver_celltypes, as.character(Celltype), "Other")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette, "Other" = "grey90")) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = "Driver cell type triad") +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank()) CTN_df %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   count(Celltype, label) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = n, y = label, fill = factor(Celltype, names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)))) +   geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = TRUE)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette, name = NULL,                     labels = rename_celltype) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 4)) +   cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Proportion of Cells", y = NULL) +   theme(plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.location = "plot",         legend.justification = "right") cluster_eval_metrics <- CTN_df %>%    mutate(label_binary = ifelse(label == "CTN1", "TLS", "Other")) %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_annot, by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   mutate(annot_binary = ifelse(Annotation_Type == "TLS", "TLS", "Other")) %>%   split(f = .$ImageID) %>%   lapply(function(df_sub) {     # ARI and NMI     ARI <- aricode::ARI(df_sub$label_binary, df_sub$annot_binary)     NMI <- aricode::NMI(df_sub$label_binary, df_sub$annot_binary)          # F-1 score     conf_mat <- caret::confusionMatrix(       data = factor(df_sub$label_binary, c("TLS", "Other")),       reference = factor(df_sub$annot_binary, c("TLS", "Other")))     F1 <- conf_mat$byClass["F1"] #for multiclass classification problems          data.frame(ARI = ARI, NMI = NMI, F1 = F1)   }) %>%   rbindlist(idcol = "ImageID")  cluster_eval_metrics #>        ImageID       ARI       NMI        F1 #>         <char>     <num>     <num>     <num> #> 1:   TILD028LA 0.7070873 0.5236775 0.7855695 #> 2:   TILD315MA 0.5718259 0.3512873 0.6826156 #> 3: VUILD104MA1 0.7288254 0.5088313 0.7753766 #> 4:  VUILD110LA 0.3925147 0.1856403 0.4319758 sessionInfo() #> R version 4.4.1 (2024-06-14) #> Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20 #> Running under: macOS 15.7.5 #>  #> Matrix products: default #> BLAS: title: Authors and Citation
text: Yufan Wu. Maintainer. Yutong Pan. Author. Lulu Shang. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pan Y, Shang L (2026). SCOPE.CTN: SCOPE (SystematiC prOfiling disease-relevant sPatial nichEs). R package version 0.0.1.
code: @Manual{,   title = {SCOPE.CTN: SCOPE (SystematiC prOfiling of disease-relevant sPatial nichEs)},   author = {Yutong Pan and Lulu Shang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1}, }

what: SCOPE (SystematiC prOfiling of disease-relevant sPatial nichEs)
title: SCOPE (SystematiC prOfiling of disease-relevant sPatial nichEs)
text: SCOPE (SystematiC prOfiling disease-relevant sPatial nichEs) scalable multi-learning statistical framework represents tissue architecture Cell type-Triad Niches (CTNs), cohort-stable motifs localized higher-order cell-type interactions. SCOPE integrates multiple spatial colocalization patterns within multi-view learning framework derive reusable niche vocabularies spatially resolved single-cell data.

title: SCOPE (SystematiC prOfiling of disease-relevant sPatial nichEs)
text: Install released version SCOPE.CTN CRAN:
code: install.packages("SCOPE.CTN")

what: Computing the between-cluster sum of squares for the linear kernels — BtwClusterSS_linear
title: Computing the between-cluster sum of squares for the linear kernels — BtwClusterSS_linear
text: Computes -cluster sum squares linear kernel associated X

title: Computing the between-cluster sum of squares for the linear kernels — BtwClusterSS_linear
code: BtwClusterSS_linear(X, H)

what: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist
title: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist
text: Define spatial neighborhood using FNN::get.knn() compute neighborhood cell counts proportions available cell types.

title: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist
code: Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(   dat_slide,   knn_num,   cell_types_available,   min.radius = 100,   max.radius = 500,   min.n.neighbors = 10,   return.nn.ids = FALSE,   prob_list = seq(0, 1, 0.25) )

title: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist
text: dat_slide Input data frame single tissue section. Columns must contain cell ids (CellID), spatial coordinates (X, Y), cell type labels (Celltype). knn_num number nearest neighbors. default value 20. cell_types_available vector length \(T\) containing available cell type labels. min.radius Lower threshold neighborhood distance boundary. default value 100. max.radius Upper threshold neighborhood distance boundary. default value 500. min.n.neighbors Minimum number nearest neighbors within neighborhood distance boundary. Cells fewer min.n.neighbors (default: 10) neighbors excluded downstream analysis. return.nn.ids Whether return cell ids nearest neighbors cell. prop.list Probabilities \([0, 1]\) produce quantiles neighborhood distance distribution

title: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist
text: list contains: count.df \(N \times T\) neighborhood cell type count matrix. Rows NaN values cells fewer fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary. prop.df \(N \times T\) neighborhood cell type proportion matrix. Rows NaN values cells fewer fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary. dist.quantiles Quantiles (including 99th percentile) distances nearest neighbors corresponding probabilities prop.list. Can used examine distribution distances. neighbor.cell.ids data frame containing cell ids nearest neighbors focal cell.

title: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist
text: neighborhood distance boundary max.dist initially set 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range (default: \([100, 500]\)), reset minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). cells fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within boundary, corresponding neighborhood cell type counts proportions set NaN subsequently excluded niche clustering.

what: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
text: Randomly shuffles cell type labels within tissue section computes permutated CLQs every cell type pair, creating null distribution compared observed CLQ compute permutation test p-value.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
code: CLQ_permutation_test(CLQ_obs, CLQ_perm)

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
text: CLQ_obs Observed CLQs computed using compute_CLQ_observed(). CLQ_perm Permutated CLQs computed using compute_CLQ_permutated().

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
text: data frame containing nominal Benjamini-Hochberg-adjusted CLQ permutation test p-values.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
text: permutation test p-value tissue section \(l\) defined $$p_{b\rightarrow }^{(l)} = \sum_{k=1}^{\mbox{iter.num}}(CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} > CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs}),$$ \(CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k}\) permutated CLQ computed using compute_CLQ_permutated() randomly shuffled cell type labels, \(CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs}\) observed CLQ computed using compute_CLQ_observed() true cell type labels.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — CLQ_permutation_test
text: Bouchard, G. et al. quantitative spatial cell-cell colocalizations framework enabling comparisons vitro assembloids pathological specimens. Nat Commun 16, 1392 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-55129-6

title: Separating cell types in CTN labels with colored dots — color_CTN_names
text: Adding colored dots cell type cell type triad (CTN) labels. Outputs can used titles labels ggplot markdown text enabled using ggtext::element_markdown.

title: Separating cell types in CTN labels with colored dots — color_CTN_names
code: color_CTN_names(   CTN,   celltype_palette = c(B = "#fee440", CD4T = "#3a86ff", CD8T = "#8338ec"),   dot_fontsize = 16,   sep = "_" )

title: Separating cell types in CTN labels with colored dots — color_CTN_names
text: CTN CTN label; e.g. B_CD4T_CD8T. celltype_palette named vector colors values cell type labels names. Must contain cell types. dot_fontsize Size dots. Default 16pt. sep character string separating cell types CTN. Note appear cell type labels.

title: Separating cell types in CTN labels with colored dots — color_CTN_names
code: library(ggplot2) library(ggtext)  CTN_colored <- color_CTN_names(   CTN = "B_CD4T_CD8T",   celltype_palette = c("B" = "#fee440", "CD4T" = "#3a86ff", "CD8T" = "#8338ec"))  ggplot() +   labs(title = CTN_colored) +   theme(plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5))

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: Computes colocation quotient (CLQ) available cell type pairs.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
code: compute_CLQ(interact_matrix, dat_in)

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: interact_matrix Input dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID Celltype.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: \(T\times T\) matrix containing CLQs every cell type pair, \(T\) number available cell types. rows matrix focal cell types columns neighboring cell types.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: colocalization pair cell types \(\) \(b\) tissue section \(l\) can evaluated CLQ metric $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow } = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} \frac{C_{b\rightarrow } / N_a}{N_b / (N - 1)} & N_a > 5 \mbox{} \ N_b > 5 \\ 0, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\), \(N_a\), \(N_b\), \(N\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: Bouchard, G. et al. quantitative spatial cell-cell colocalizations framework enabling comparisons vitro assembloids pathological specimens. Nat Commun 16, 1392 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-55129-6

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: Computes observed colocation quotient (CLQ) available cell type pairs tissue section spatial omics dataset.

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
code: compute_CLQ_observed(   cell_neighbor_counts,   dat_in,   min.ncell = 100,   num_cores = 1 )

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: cell_neighbor_counts \(N \times T\) neighborhood cell type count matrix obtained using Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(). dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID, ImageID Celltype. min.ncell Minimum cell number threshold. Tissue sections fewer min.ncell cells excluded analysis. num_cores number cores use pbmcapply::pbmclapply(), .e. many child processes run simultaneously. Can set 1 Windows.

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: data frame \(T\times T\times L\) rows containing observed CLQs focal (Celltype) neighboring cell types (Celltype_neighbor) tissue section (ImageID), \(T\) number available cell types \(L\) number tissue sections.

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: colocalization pair cell types \(\) \(b\) tissue section \(l\) can evaluated observed CLQ $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & N_a^{(l)} \leq 5 \mbox{ } \ N_b^{(l)} \leq 5 \\ \frac{C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)}, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs}\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\), \(N_a^{(l)}\), \(N_b^{(l)}\), \(N^{(l)}\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: Bouchard, G. et al. quantitative spatial cell-cell colocalizations framework enabling comparisons vitro assembloids pathological specimens. Nat Commun 16, 1392 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-55129-6

what: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: Randomly shuffles cell type labels within tissue section computes permutated CLQs every cell type pair, creating null distribution compared observed CLQ compute p-value.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
code: compute_CLQ_permutated(   cell_neighbor_ids,   dat_in,   min.ncell = 100,   num_cores = 5,   iter.num = 500,   seed = 42 )

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: cell_neighbor_ids Cell IDs nearest neighbors obtained using Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(). dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID, ImageID Celltype. min.ncell Minimum cell number threshold. Tissue sections fewer min.ncell cells excluded analysis. iter.num Number permutations (default: 500). seed Random seed passed base::set.seed().

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: data frame \(T\times T\times L\times \mbox{iter.num}\) rows containing permutated CLQs focal (Celltype) neighboring cell types (Celltype_neighbor) tissue section (ImageID), \(T\) number available cell types \(L\) number tissue sections.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: tissue section, cell type labels randomly shuffled iter.num (default: 500) times generate permutated CLQ values $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & N_a^{(l)} \leq 5 \mbox{ } \ N_b^{(l)} \leq 5 \\ \frac{C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)}, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k}\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\) iteration \(k, k=\)1,2,...,iter.num, \(N_a^{(l)}\), \(N_b^{(l)}\), \(N^{(l)}\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: Bouchard, G. et al. quantitative spatial cell-cell colocalizations framework enabling comparisons vitro assembloids pathological specimens. Nat Commun 16, 1392 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-55129-6

code: compute_H_linear(X_m_list, theta, centers)

what: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
title: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
text: internal function adapted MKKC::mkkcEst performing robust multiple kernel K-means clustering multiview data.

title: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
code: mkkcEst_linear(   X_m_list,   centers,   iter.max = 100,   epsilon = 1e-04,   theta = rep(1/length(X_m_list), length(X_m_list)),   alpha = 0.5 )

title: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
text: X_m_list list length \(P\) (default: 4) containing \(P\) kernel matrices. centers number clusters \(K\). iter.max maximum number iterations allowed. default 100. epsilon Convergence threshold. default \(10^{-4}\). theta Initial values kernel coefficients. default 1/P views. alpha Decay rate used computing exponential moving average \(\theta\). interation \(\scriptl\), \({\theta^{(\scriptl)}}^{EMA} = \alpha \theta^{(\scriptl)} + (1-\alpha) {\theta^{(\scriptl-1)}}^{EMA}, \alpha\[0, 1]\). default value 0.5.

title: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
text: list contains: cluster vector integers (1:K) indicating cluster point allocated. totss total sum squares. withinss Matrix within-cluster sum squares cluster, one row per view. withinsscluster Vector within-cluster sum squares, one component per cluster. withinssview Vector within-cluster sum squares, one component per view. tot.withinss Total within-cluster sum squares, .e. sum(withinsscluster). betweenssview Vector  -cluster sum squares, one component per view. tot.betweenss -cluster sum squares, .e. totss-tot.withinss. clustercount number clusters K. coefficients kernel coefficients H continuous clustering assignment size number points, one component per cluster. iter number iterations. MbatchKm_WCSS_per_cluster Within-cluster sum squares cluster recovering cluster labels final H using minibatch K-means. MbatchKm_centroids Final cluster centroids.

what: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
text: Non-linear version SCOPE used cell type triad niche (CTN) screening.

title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
code: run_SCOPE(   combination_table,   cell_neighbor_table,   dat_in,   clusternum = 2,   min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE,   mgcv_df = 15,   iter.max = 100,   epsilon = 1e-04,   alpha = 0.5,   num_cores = 1,   rename_CTN = TRUE,   save_results = TRUE,   output_dir = getwd(),   suffix = "" )

title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
text: combination_table Data frame containing cell type combinations CTN screening. row combination. cell_neighbor_table \(N\times T\) neighborhood cell type proportion table. Column names must contain cell type labels combination_table. dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID Celltype. clusternum number clusters \(K\). default value 2. min.prop Minimum neighborhood cell type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0. default value 0.3. linear Whether run linear version SCOPE (.e. applying thin plate regression spline smoothing). Default FALSE. mgcv_df dimension basis used represent thin plate regression spline smooth term. default 15. See mgcv::s details. Disabled linear=TRUE. iter.max maximum number iterations allowed. default 100. epsilon Convergence threshold. default \(10^{-4}\). alpha Decay rate \(\alpha\) used computing exponential moving average weight vector \(\theta\). interation \(l\), \({\theta^{(l)}}^{EMA} = \alpha \theta^{(l)} + (1-\alpha) {\theta^{(l-1)}}^{EMA}, \alpha\[0, 1]\). default value 0.5. num_cores number cores use pbmcapply::pbmclapply, .e. many child processes run simultaneously. Can set 1 Windows. rename_CTN Whether rename niches based proportion driver cell types. cluster highest proportion named CTN clusternum = 2 CTN1 otherwise. cluster lowest driver cell type proportion renamed Unassigned. clusters named CTN2, ... accordingly clusternum > 2. save_results Whether save niche screening results output directory. TRUE (default) results CTN saved separate file. FALSE CTN screening results combined single data frame returned function output. output_dir Output directory. automatically create new directory target directory exist. suffix File name suffix.

title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
text: res CTN screening results. returns save_results FALSE.
